schedule_metadata.agency_name: Department for Aging and Rehabilitative Services

series_metadata.series_title: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program (LTCOP) Activities and Case Records
series_metadata.series_description: This series documents the provision of various services by state and local staff within the Office of the State Long-Term Care Ombudsman (LTCOP) to older persons receiving long-term care services, including non-casework activities to promote resident's rights and improve quality of care; and the receipt, investigation, and resolution of complaints. This series may include, but is not limited to: releases of information, complaints, letters, and case work documentation.

retention_rules.disposition: 5 Years after end of federal fiscal, year Confidential Destruction

schedule_metadata.agency_name: Department for Aging and Rehabilitative Services

series_metadata.series_description: This series documents a client's information and access to longterm support and disability programs and benefits through the No Wrong Door system. This series may include, but is not limited to: Uniform Assessment Instrument (UAI) and consent forms.

retention_rules.disposition: 10 Years after last action, Confidential Destruction

schedule_metadata.agency_name: Department for Aging and Rehabilitative Services

series_metadata.series_description: This series documents a client's disability and yearly evaluations of the client receiving personal assistance services. This series may include, but is not limited to applications, medical and vocational assessments, Personal Assistance Services assessment(PAS), and financial information.

schedule_metadata.agency_name: Department for Aging and Rehabilitative Services

series_metadata.series_description: This series documents start up packets for personal assistants hired to help individuals with disabilities with non-medical activities of daily living. This series may include, but is not limited to: personal assistant provider forms, personal assistant agreements, I-9 forms and documentation, consumer notification forms, direct deposit authorization agreements, W-4 forms and VA-4 forms.

retention_rules.disposition: 5 Years after last action, Confidential Destruction

schedule_metadata.agency_name: Department for Aging and Rehabilitative Services

series_metadata.series_description: This series documents a client's request for agency services and the agency's actions and responses made to the request. This series may include, but is not limited to: intake applications, payment requests, invoice authorizations, transportation assessments, monthly vendor reports, lien worksheets, and referral information.
